subject: Biology
title: The Cell Wall
description: A fast breakdown of The Cell Wall for builders.

hook.text: Plant cells have something animal cells don't—a rigid wall outside the membrane.

buildup.text: Made of cellulose, the cell wall gives plants their structure and prevents them from bursting.

discovery.text: Wood is mostly dead cells whose cellulose walls remain standing long after the cell dies.

twist.text: Fungi have cell walls too, but theirs are chitin—the same material in insect shells.

climax.text: Penicillin kills bacteria by blocking cell wall construction. No wall, no survival.

punchline.text: Plants build walls. Animals never do.

quiz.question: What are plant cell walls primarily made of?
